name: Mobile application
summary: A user-installed native or cross-platform application running on iOS/Android handsets and tablets, often offline-capable and sync-backed. Constrained by device resources, OS sandboxes, app-store gatekeeping, and highly variable networks.

concerns: touch-first interaction: hit-target sizes, gestures, thumb reach, one-handed use
offline-state affordances: clear indication of stale/unsynced data and queued actions
interruption resilience (calls, backgrounding, low-memory kills) without losing user input
first-run onboarding and permission priming before the OS permission prompt
small-viewport information density; orientation and dynamic-type support
questions: Is the offline/unsynced state made visible to the user and are queued actions recoverable?
Is user input preserved across backgrounding, interruptions, and OS-initiated kills?
Are OS permissions requested in context with prior priming rather than at launch?

concerns: offline-first operation and background/foreground sync with conflict resolution (last-write-wins vs merge/CRDT)
crash-free-session rate targets and ANR/watchdog-termination limits
graceful behaviour on intermittent, flaky, or transitioning networks (Wi-Fi<->cellular)
safe retry/queue of mutating requests with idempotency keys to avoid duplicates
recovery of local state after force-quit, OS update, or low-storage eviction
questions: Is the sync model and its conflict-resolution strategy specified for concurrent edits across devices?
Are crash-free-session and ANR targets quantified?
Are mutating operations idempotent so retries over flaky networks don't duplicate effects?

concerns: secure local storage of secrets/tokens in Keychain/Keystore, not plist/SharedPrefs; encryption of app data at rest
certificate pinning and TLS to defend against MITM on hostile networks
biometric/device auth (Face ID / fingerprint) with secure-enclave-backed keys and fallback
protection of deep links / URL schemes against interception and injection; universal-link validation
resistance to reverse engineering, rooted/jailbroken devices, tampering, and screen-capture of sensitive views
OAuth/token refresh with secure session handling and remote revocation
questions: Are tokens and secrets stored in the platform keystore/keychain rather than insecure app storage?
Is transport secured with pinning against MITM, and are deep links validated against injection?
Is behaviour on rooted/jailbroken or tampered devices specified (per OWASP MASVS)?

concerns: cold/warm start time and time-to-interactive budgets
battery drain: background activity, wake-locks, location polling frequency
memory footprint to avoid OS low-memory termination on low-end devices
network/data-usage efficiency (payload size, compression, cellular vs Wi-Fi, metered-connection awareness)
scroll/animation smoothness (jank, dropped frames, 60/120fps targets)
app binary/download size and its effect on install conversion
questions: Are cold-start, frame-rate, and memory budgets quantified for the low-end target device?
Are battery and background-activity limits specified (location cadence, wake-locks)?
Is cellular/metered data usage bounded, with graceful behaviour on slow networks?

concerns: OS-version fragmentation and minimum-supported-version policy (iOS/Android range)
device fragmentation: screen sizes/densities, notches/cutouts, foldables, tablets
backward compatibility of the sync API with older app versions still in the field
forced-vs-optional update strategy for shipping breaking changes
hardware-capability variance (camera, NFC, sensors, biometrics) with graceful fallback
questions: Is the supported OS-version and device matrix specified, including foldables/tablets?
Does the back-end remain compatible with older app versions still installed in the field?
Is a forced-vs-optional update strategy defined for breaking changes?

concerns: app-store review & release pipeline (Apple/Google review latency, staged/phased rollout, rollback via halt not recall)
privacy nutrition labels / data-safety form accuracy as a release gate
over-the-air config and feature flags to control shipped binaries (kill switches)
crash/analytics telemetry (Crashlytics/Sentry) with symbolication and consented collection
code-push/OTA-update constraints per store policy; certificate/provisioning-profile and signing-key management
questions: Is a phased-rollout with monitoring and a remote kill switch defined, given you cannot instantly recall a build?
Are store privacy-label / data-safety declarations kept accurate as a release gate?
Are signing keys and provisioning profiles managed and recoverable?

concerns: App Tracking Transparency (ATT) consent before IDFA/advertising-identifier access; no tracking without opt-in
accurate App Store privacy labels / Play data-safety disclosures matching actual SDK behaviour
device-permission justification strings and least-privilege access (location, contacts, photos, mic)
third-party SDK data collection (ads/analytics) disclosed and consented; children's privacy (COPPA/age gating) where relevant
GDPR/CCPA consent, data-subject rights, and on-device PII handling
questions: Is tracking gated behind ATT consent and are advertising identifiers used only after opt-in?
Do declared privacy labels match what the app and its SDKs actually collect?
Are device permissions least-privilege with in-context purpose justification?

concerns: platform screen-reader support (VoiceOver / TalkBack) with proper accessibility labels and traits
Dynamic Type / font-scaling and reflow without truncation or clipping
sufficient contrast, reduced-motion, and color-independent cues
adequate touch-target sizes and focus order for switch control
WCAG 2.2 AA / platform accessibility guideline conformance
questions: Is VoiceOver/TalkBack support with correct labels required across the primary flows?
Does the UI support Dynamic Type / font scaling without clipping?
Is a WCAG 2.2 AA / platform accessibility conformance level required?

concerns: local datastore schema and migrations across app-version upgrades (Core Data / Room / SQLite)
sync payload data model and delta/tombstone handling for deletes
on-device data retention, cache eviction, and secure wipe on logout/uninstall
data classification for what may be cached locally vs must stay server-side
encryption of the local store and backup-exclusion of sensitive files
questions: Are local-store schema migrations defined so app upgrades don't corrupt or lose data?
Is on-device retention and secure wipe on logout/uninstall specified?
Is it defined which data may be cached on-device versus kept server-only?

concerns: single-codebase vs per-platform parity for cross-platform frameworks (feature drift between iOS/Android)
packaging & distribution channels (App Store, Play, TestFlight, enterprise/MDM, sideload)
adaptation to new form factors (foldables, tablets, wearables, CarPlay/Android Auto)
install/upgrade/rollback constraints imposed by the stores
questions: Is feature parity across iOS and Android specified, or is drift acceptable?
Are the distribution channels (stores, enterprise/MDM, TestFlight) defined?
